evals.name: springboot-java-s3-happy-path
evals.prompt: Setup NonTransactionalTargetSystem for my Spring Boot Java app to manage AWS S3 changes using Maven.
evals.expected_output: A Spring Boot bean wiring a NonTransactionalTargetSystem with S3 dependencies and named properties.
expectations: Includes flamingock-community and an external SDK dependency
Warns that this target system cannot back the AuditStore

evals.name: standalone-kotlin-gradle-with-properties
evals.prompt: I need to configure Flamingock for a standalone Kotlin project with Gradle to manage Kafka Schema Registry. Show how to register the client and compatibility level.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin snippet creating a NonTransactionalTargetSystem with addDependency and setProperty calls, plus Gradle configuration.
expectations: Configures community() instead of a technology-specific Flamingock module

evals.name: existing-client-reuse
evals.prompt: Flamingock is already in my Spring Boot app and I already have an S3Client bean. Wire NonTransactionalTargetSystem without creating another client.
evals.expected_output: A Spring Boot NonTransactionalTargetSystem bean that reuses the existing client bean.
expectations: Does not create a second client bean
Stays in the targetsystem skill boundary

evals.name: multiple-dependency-ambiguity
evals.prompt: I have two KafkaTemplate beans in my Spring Boot app. Help me wire a NonTransactionalTargetSystem for notifications.
evals.expected_output: A response that flags the multiple-dependency ambiguity and shows how to register them by name instead of inventing a selection.
expectations: Keeps the answer focused on NonTransactionalTargetSystem wiring

evals.name: targetsystem-hand-off-to-change-skill
evals.prompt: My NonTransactionalTargetSystem is already configured. Now create a migration to tag an S3 bucket.
evals.expected_output: Either a hand-off to the non-transactional change skill or migration-oriented help that assumes the TargetSystem is already configured.
expectations: Either redirects to flamingock-non-transactional-change or gives migration-oriented help
Does not pretend onboarding or targetsystem setup is still needed
Keeps the response focused on migration authoring rather than redoing targetsystem wiring
